resources.string: See today\'s calories and macros at a glance.
See today\'s protein progress at a glance.
See today\'s water progress at a glance.
Enable Water Tracking in Fud AI
Fud AI widget needs a refresh. Open Fud AI.
Mostly seated at work and home; little or no planned exercise.\nApprox. step guide: under 5,000 steps/day.
Mostly seated; light exercise or casual activity 1–3 days/week.\nApprox. step guide: 5,000–7,499 steps/day.
Regular gym, cardio, climbing, cycling, or sport 3–5 days/week.\nApprox. step guide: 7,500–9,999 steps/day.
Training most days, or a job with substantial standing, movement, or lifting.\nApprox. step guide: 10,000–12,499 steps/day.
Hard training 6–7 days/week, endurance training, or demanding physical work.\nApprox. step guide: 12,500–14,999 steps/day.
Competitive/high-volume athlete, twice-daily training, or heavy manual work plus frequent training.\nApprox. step guide: 15,000+ steps/day.
API key (or anything if endpoint doesn\'t need one)
Android\'s on-device speech recognition. Free, works offline on most phones, real-time partial results. Recommended default.
Gemini API audio transcription. Uses batch audio understanding, not Google Cloud\'s real-time Speech-to-Text API.
OpenAI Whisper API. High accuracy, 99+ languages, paid per minute.
Groq-hosted Whisper Large v3. Very fast inference, has a free tier.
Deepgram Nova. Real-time and batch modes, fast and accurate.
AssemblyAI Universal model. Strong accuracy, free tier available.
Just snap, track, and thrive.\nYour nutrition, simplified.
This helps us calculate your metabolism
Used to calculate your daily needs
Choose based on your average week, including work and exercise.
Helps us calculate your metabolism more accurately
No worries! We\'ll use a standard formula\nbased on your height, weight, and age.
You can set this later in Settings.
Log your first body fat % to see trends
How fast do you want\nto reach your goal?
We\'ll keep your calories steady\nto maintain your current weight.
Gentle and sustainable. Great for long-term habits.
Aggressive but doable. Requires strong discipline.
The most balanced pace, motivating and sustainable.
Get gentle reminders at meal times\nso you never forget to track.
You can change this anytime in Settings.
Keep your nutrition and body\nmeasurements in sync automatically.
Fud AI needs an AI provider key to\nanalyze your food. You bring your own.
Get a free key at aistudio.google.com/apikey
13 providers supported. Your key stays on\nthis device, encrypted.
The minimum recommendation is 1,200 calories per day.
2 eggs, toast with butter and a coffee
Chipotle burrito bowl with chicken and rice
Greek yogurt with granola and blueberries
Preview how this meal changes today before logging it.
e.g. no oil, extra cheese, large portion
Couldn\'t start the mic. Check permissions.
No favorites yet\nSwipe left on any food to add it
Your coach can see your weight history, calorie log, and goals. Ask about expected weight, what to eat, or how to hit your target.
Clear all messages and start fresh? This can\'t be undone.
No profile yet. Finish onboarding first.
What\'s my expected weight in 30 days?
How do I lose weight faster safely?
What should I eat for dinner?
How do I gain weight healthily?
How am I doing this week?
Any advice based on my log?
Log your first weight to see trends
%1$d calculated burns · tap to view or delete
This removes the calculated burn from Fud AI and Health Connect. Your workout diary plan stays saved.
%1$d entries · tap to view or delete
You\'ve reached your goal weight! Head to Settings to switch your goal and tap Recalculate Goals to refresh your targets.
Strength 1–10: 1 is very easy and 10 is maximum effort.\nCR10 0–10: perceived exertion with decimals allowed.\nBorg 6–20: classic whole-number exertion scale.
On = Katch-McArdle (uses lean mass for a more accurate BMR). Off = Mifflin-St Jeor (height/weight/age only). Turn off if your body-fat reading is outdated.
Changes the main app color used for tabs, buttons, icons, charts, and progress rings.
New food review sheets open with grams selected first. You can still switch to serving, slice, cup, or any other detected unit before logging.
Set when automatic meal selection switches to the next meal.
Each meal continues until the next one starts. Late Snack continues overnight until Breakfast starts. You can still change the meal manually before logging.
Scroll in 50 ml steps to select a daily goal from 50 to 10,000 ml.
Scroll in 1 fl oz steps to select a daily goal from 2 to 338 fl oz.
e.g. I live in Germany, assume European portion sizes, prefer ALDI/REWE brands. I\'m on a bodybuilding cut.
Optional context sent with every AI request — region, diet, athletic goals, anything you\'d otherwise repeat each time. Leave empty to disable.
If your primary provider fails (overload, rate limit, network error), the app retries automatically with this fallback. Same provider as primary is allowed — just pick a different model.

When on, Fud AI uses your measured calories burned from Health Connect — a 14-day average of active + basal energy — as your maintenance anchor when calculating goals, instead of the formula estimate. No AI is used to read your burn. Requires Health Connect. Works with the Recalculate button and with Adaptive Goals.

About once a week when you open the app, Fud AI automatically re-runs the full goal calculation — the same one the Recalculate button uses — from your profile, recent logged food, and weight trend. If Energy Burn is on, it uses your measured burn as the maintenance anchor. It skips silently if the AI is unavailable. Turning this off restores the targets from before Adaptive Goals first changed them. This is not medical advice.
Wipes all food entries and photos. Profile, weights, and settings stay.
Wipes profile, food log, weight history, chat, and API keys, then takes you back to onboarding. Health Connect data stays intact.
At most 2 macros can be locked at a time, so one stays free to balance. Unlock another macro first (tap its lock icon).
Calories is locked and both other macros are locked, so there\'s nothing left to absorb this change. Unlock calories or another macro, then try again.
Turn off Adaptive Goals to lock or set your own calories and macros. While it\'s on, Fud AI recalculates them for you each week.
Resets calories and macros to formula defaults from your height/weight/activity/goal. Any pinned macros are cleared.
Goal weight doesn\'t match your goal
A Lose goal needs a target below your current weight.
A Gain goal needs a target above your current weight.
Notifications permission denied. Enable it in System Settings → Apps → Fud AI → Notifications to receive reminders.
Log today\'s weight to keep your progress chart accurate.
Log today\'s body fat % to track your composition trend.
Health Connect did not grant access. Open Health Connect and allow the data types you want Fud AI to sync. Energy-based goals require both Active and Total Calories.
Health Connect isn\'t available on this device. Install it from the Play Store, then re-tap the toggle.
Or type any custom model ID below.
Pin up to 2 macros to exact grams. Unpinned ones auto-balance from remaining calories.
Let the provider detect the language.

I\'ve been tracking my meals with Fud AI — snap a photo, speak it, or type it, and the AI logs the calories. It\'s free, open source, and your data stays on your device.\n\nDownload: https://fud-ai.app
Daily reminder to log a meal and keep your streak.
End-of-day summary of your calories + macros.
Celebration when you reach your goal weight.
Don\'t forget to log your meals
Keep your streak going — log something from today.
Tap to see how today\'s macros lined up.
See today\'s calories, protein, carbs, and fat in one widget.
Try a different muscle, equipment, or search — or reset the filters above.
Your plan auto-adjusts weekly as you log — turn off Adaptive Goals in Settings to keep it fixed.
Snap a photo — AI logs it
New Workouts tab — browse 873 exercises with photos, muscle and equipment filters, search, and detail pages.
Home redesign: speedometer calorie gauge, vertical macro bars, four nutrient cards, and a floating add button.
Ask Coach by voice with the new inline recorder.
Share any meal as a link that opens straight in Fud AI on iPhone or Android.
Export your food diary as JSON, Markdown, or CSV from Settings.
Reprocess a logged meal with AI — adjust its name, serving, or note and re-analyze.
Camera + Camera keeps both shots, stitched side by side.
10 new theme colors, now picked inline in Settings.
Body fat history: review and delete individual entries, just like weight.
Height and weight units can now be set independently.
Adaptive Goals and Energy Burn left Experimental and are on by default for new installs.
Widgets redesign: the Home speedometer style, drawn at each widget\'s true size.
Reinstalled or switched phones? Your food log, weight, and body fat now restore automatically from Health Connect.
Hold to record, tap to lock
No foods logged on this day
Add a note to refine this entry — e.g. large bowl, extra olive oil — then tap Reprocess
Pick exactly four nutrients for the Home summary row.
Finish onboarding first to compare this meal against your daily goals.
No logged meals in the selected range.
No notification type is selected. You can leave notifications allowed and keep every app reminder off.
Pin up to 2 macros to exact grams. Unpinned ones auto-balance from remaining calories.
Adaptive Goals is on. Fud AI will check once per week after enough food and weight data exists.
Adds to your log with the exact nutrients from the sender. No photo is included.
Point the camera at the barcode
Packaged foods use Open Food Facts when available.
Daily reminder to weigh in and log your weight.
Daily reminder to log a body-fat reading.
A new version of Fud AI is available on the Play Store.
Daily reminder to drink and log water.
Have some water and log it in Fud AI.
A new version of Fud AI is ready. Tap to update.

Your daily calorie and macro targets are set by AI. When you tap Recalculate Goals — or automatically about once a week if Adaptive Goals is on — Fud AI sends your profile, the reference equations below, your recently logged food, and your weight trend to your AI provider. It starts from these peer-reviewed formulas, then adjusts them to your real data to estimate your true maintenance and targets.
Default formula for resting metabolism. Used when you haven\'t entered a body fat %.
Used automatically when you\'ve entered a body fat %. More accurate for lean and athletic users since it derives BMR from lean body mass instead of total weight.
TDEE = BMR × activity multiplier. The multiplier corresponds to your selected activity level. This is the maintenance baseline the AI starts from (unless Energy Burn supplies a measured one).
Sedentary: 1.2 · Light: 1.375 · Moderate: 1.465 · Active: 1.55 · Very Active: 1.725 · Extra Active: 1.9

Maintenance starts from your TDEE — or, when Energy Burn is on, your measured Health Connect burn (a 14-day Active + Basal average) instead of the formula estimate. The AI refines maintenance against your logged intake and weight trend, then applies your goal: a weekly weight-change rate becomes a daily calorie deficit (Lose) or surplus (Gain).
1 lb of body fat ≈ 3,500 kcal · 1 kg ≈ 7,700 kcal\nYour weekly rate ÷ 7 is subtracted from (Lose) or added to (Gain) maintenance to set the daily calorie target.

The AI fits protein, carbs, and fat to your calorie target, using these references as a guide. Protein scales with your activity level (and is based on lean body mass when a body fat %% is set); fat is set from bodyweight; carbs fill the rest. You can lock any value or edit it yourself in Settings.
Protein: ~0.8–2.2 g per kg by activity level (raised slightly when losing)\nFat: ~0.6 g per kg bodyweight\nCarbs: remaining calories ÷ 4 kcal/g

Calorie, macro, fiber, sugar, saturated fat, cholesterol, sodium, potassium and other micronutrient values returned per meal are AI-generated estimates from the food image, voice transcript, or text description, using the AI provider you selected.

Fud AI is an estimation tool, not a clinical instrument. Predictive equations carry inherent error (typically ±10%% for BMR). Consult a registered dietitian, physician, or sports medicine professional before significant diet changes — especially if you have a medical condition, are pregnant or breastfeeding, are under 18, or are managing an eating disorder.
Allow Fud AI to read your Active and Total Calories in Health Connect, then turn Energy Burn on again.
Fud AI needs at least 3 recent days of Health Connect energy data before it can use your measured burn.
